name: Non-invasive ventilation
description: Patient on non-invasive ventilation (via mask/interface, no tube); the act only — ventilation modes are separate.

en.name: Non-invasive ventilation
en.shortDescription: Patient on non-invasive ventilation (via mask/interface, no tube); the act only — ventilation modes are separate.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Non-invasive ventilation (NIV) is breathing support delivered through an external interface — a face or nasal mask, helmet, or similar — without an artificial airway inside the trachea. "Non-invasive" refers to that interface, as opposed to invasive ventilation, which is delivered through an endotracheal or tracheostomy tube. In source data this is a **procedure** record indicating that the patient was on non-invasive ventilation; there is no unit. It is recorded in intensive care, respiratory wards, and emergency settings.

## Included Concepts

This set captures the act of non-invasive ventilation itself:

- *4177224 / 428311008 — Non-invasive ventilation (SNOMED)*: the general concept; the default for "patient on NIV".
- *40486624 / 447837008 — Non-invasive positive pressure ventilation (SNOMED)*: NIV delivering positive pressure (the usual form).

The **ventilation mode** used non-invasively (CPAP, bilevel/BiPAP, pressure support, spontaneous-timed, etc.) is a separate dimension — modes apply to invasive and non-invasive ventilation alike — and is captured in the ventilation-mode concept sets (in particular the spontaneous/support modes set), not here.

## Mapping Notes

- Map a record stating the patient was on non-invasive ventilation (via mask/interface) to *4177224 / 428311008 — Non-invasive ventilation*, or *40486624 / 447837008 — Non-invasive positive pressure ventilation* when positive-pressure NIV is specified.
- **Mode is separate**: do not map a specific mode (CPAP, BiPAP/bilevel, pressure support, spontaneous-timed) to this set — those belong to the ventilation-mode concept sets. A patient can be on NIV *and* a specific mode at the same time; record each in its own set.
- **Invasive vs non-invasive**: ventilation delivered through an endotracheal or tracheostomy tube is *invasive* and belongs to the Invasive mechanical ventilation concept set, not here.
- **Weaning from NIV** is a distinct event (part of weaning) and is not the NIV act; it is not included in this set.

fr.name: Ventilation non invasive
fr.shortDescription: Patient sous ventilation non invasive (par masque/interface, sans sonde) ; le geste seul — les modes ventilatoires sont à part.

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Reduce to the non-invasive ventilation act only (4177224 NIV, 40486624 NIPPV). Moved the NIV ventilatory modes (CPAP, BiPAP/dual-pressure, spontaneous-timed S/T, spontaneous-with-support, nocturnal CPAP) to the Spontaneous/support ventilation-mode set 357. Removed Weaning from NIV (3655902) and CPAP to non-ventilated lung (40488414). Added clinical description.
